title: chrysanthemum orientale
intro: Chrysanthemum orientale, also known as the Korean chrysanthemum or Dang Gui, is a flowering plant native to East Asia, particularly in China, Korea, and Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including improving cardiovascular health, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and depression. Chrysanthemum orientale can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional herbal preparations.
